mechanics.text:
CHARACTER CREATION
All Attributes start with minimum value of 2 except for Social Standing_ which has minimum of This minimum can be raised by the character affiliation and Life Path; Use the highest minimum listed for the Attribute; if subsequent path assigns lower minimum than one you already have disregard it, An exception to this rule is the Social Standing minimum for Clan characters see Attribute minimums can never be lower than higher tnan 8. Also Attribute minimums only apply during character creation. After play starts these minimums are irrelevant.
Variable Level Traits Many traits have several levels; For example, character can have from one to three levels of the Well-Connected Trait (see 891. Levels are variations in trait' Iisted cost Or value For example the three levels of Well Connected cost 2 , and 6 points respectively: When variable trait is awarded in the Life Path, the char- acter receives at the lowest level  listed_ unless the path states otherwise If the character gets trait ne or she already has; increase its level by one. For example character who already has Well-Connected at cost f would get it again at cost of 4_ the next level Multiple levels are given parentheses: for example Wealth (2). In this case the character' initial level of Wealth would be or increased by 2 if he already has the trait. Any additional increases beyond the   highest level   listed not count, unless the trait can be obtained twice_ In this case_ the character receives second time, starting at the lowest level:
Attribute Thresholds The life your character leads will influence certain Attributes_ Though you can choose   specific Attribute values their cost may be affected by Attribute thresholds listed in the Life Path. higher  threshold makes easier have that Attribute at higher value _ while lower threshold makes harder t0 have high Attribute value_ AlI Attributes start with threshold of 6 except for Edge which begins at threshold of
Rank This special trait represents character' = military rank and is   listed the Life Path Promotion or   Demotion. Promotion increases the character 5 rank_ while Demotion decreases character'$ rank cannot rise above 10 or drop below character" Rank Trait starts off as enlisted (as opposed to officer; see the Rank Trait description 851. If the charac ter earns commission (gains the Commission Trait) , his or her rank drops to (unless stated otherwise but switches the char- acter t0 the lowest officer rank:
Tne Life  Path and events tell you when t0 increase decrease a Attribute threshold by listing the Attribute' abbre viation and positive negative modifier; For example_ your character's first path lists STR +1 and BOD -1 this tells you to increase your character's Strength threshold by point (to and reduce the Body threshold by (to 5). These modifiers do not apply directly to Attribute values instead_ they increase reduce the number of points it may cost you to give your char acter certain Attribute value (see Assigning Attributes, Traits and Skills_ P: 541. Attribute   threshold cannot drop below increase higher than 10. Disregard modifiers that exceed these limits
Opposing Traits Some traits are diametrically opposed t0 one another. For example certain Life Path may grant poverty-stricken char- acter the Wealth Trait: Any time character receives trait mutually exclusive of trait he or she already has the traits cancel each other out. character with the Poverty Trait who receives the Wealth Trait does not become wealthy; but instead loses the Poverty Trait_ If the character gets Wealth again later in the Life Path, he then receives the Wealth Trait. character gets an opposing trait with variable levels reduce the level of the opposing trait by one rather than elimi- nating it entirely: For example character has Wealth (2 and the Life Path instructs him t0 take on Poverty; his Wealth is reduced t0
ACQUIRING TRAITS Traits   represent character'$   special abilities draw backs that are beyond the scope of Attributes and skills such as Ambidexterity Poor Hearing; character need not have traits t0 be interesting or powerful; traits are simply intended to add spice character beyond what his or her skills and Attributes define_ Characters gain most traits through the Life Path though players can add or remove some along with assigning their char: acters specific Attributes and skills_ complete Iist of traits and what they do appears on Some Life Paths endow character with positive Or nega tive traits based on the circumstances of the path and any events that transpire character already has the trait in question, the player should generally disregard it. If the trait has variable levels (see below), or if character can have it more than once (such as Contact or Enemy), the player may choose to take the trait at higher level or second trait of the same type_ as appropriate t0 the character'$ story: Traits awarded to character during the Life Path cost noth: ing: Players can purchase additional   traits when assigning Attributes _ traits and skills
LEARNING Skills Throughout his her Life Path your character will learn wide variety of skills_ which represent specific areas of knowledge and/or ability: Tne types of skills and the character' = level of pro ficiency depend on the choices you make and the random events life throws in your character' way: complete Iist of skills and what they do appears in the Skills chapter, beginning on All characters start with Language and Perception Skills_ described in Stage of the Life Path (p. 331.
